title: Tohoku Earthy Beef Kaiseki Course
description: A traditional Japanese Kaiseki course featuring earthy and crunchy elements, with a focus on rest-meat and macrobiotic-friendly ingredients, finished with a touch of powdered sugar.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the Wagyu beef with salt and pepper, then sear it in a hot skillet with sesame oil until browned on all sides.
Transfer the beef to the oven and roast for 20 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 130 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the beef is cooking, saute the rehydrated shiitake mushrooms in a separate skillet with soy sauce and mirin until they are tender and caramelized.
Add the sliced lotus root and cubed Japanese pumpkin to the skillet with the mushrooms and cook until they are tender and lightly browned.
Remove the beef from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
To serve, place a slice of the rested beef on each plate, then spoon the mushroom and vegetable mixture over the top.
Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and a sprinkle of powdered sugar.
